title: Segregation of duties and toxic combinations
description: SoD splits initiate, authorize, and execute across principals so no one can both commit and conceal a sensitive action.
body: Use when one role can drive a sensitive workflow end to end. SoD requires that initiating, authorizing, and executing a sensitive operation are held by different principals, so that fraud or error needs collusion rather than a single actor. A 'toxic combination' is a permission set that recombines two conflicting steps (e.g. create a vendor and approve its payment, or grant elevated access and use it). Do not assume that because each individual permission seems reasonable, holding both is safe. Actions: enumerate conflicting pairs and scan role assignments for principals holding both halves. Validation signals include a test that the same principal cannot both create and approve a request. Risk boundary: an unresolved toxic combination on a financial or access-control workflow is a reportable control deficiency; escalate.
